name: Prosciutto with Carmalized Pears
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
12 thin slices prosciutto (Prosciutto di Parma preferred)
1/4 cup toasted pine nuts or walnuts
recipeInstructions:
( Available at Italian markets and some specialty food stores)
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Wash and core the pears, then slice them in half lengthwise.
3. Rub each pear half all over with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
4. Place cut sides down on a heavy baking sheet and roast for 40-45 minutes until soft and caramelized.
5. In a bowl or food processor, mix blue cheese and mascarpone until well combined. Season with salt and pepper if desired. Set aside.
6. Arrange two slices of prosciutto on each plate and place a watercress sprig beside them.
7. When the pears are tender, remove from oven and discard cores.
8. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of cheese mixture into each pear half.
9. Return to oven for 5-7 minutes until cheese melts.
10. Slice pear halves in half again and place on plates with prosciutto.
11. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with nuts.
12. Serve immediately with a fruity red wine such as Dolcetto.
